rag_rules: Repair only AGREE clauses with type mismatches or unresolved identifiers by aligning expressions to visible AADL features or deleting invalid ones.
Do not introduce new declarations or modify architecture to resolve AGREE type errors; use only existing ports, subcomponents, and bindings.
generic_rules: Ensure all expressions in AGREE contracts use types consistent with their source: Boolean expressions for bool, numeric for int/real.
Implementation-level bindings must appear in implementation annexes, not component type annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE scalar types are int, real, bool—never integer, boolean, or package-qualified variants.
Comparison operators (=>) require both sides to be of type bool; avoid numeric or opaque data in Boolean contexts.
anti_patterns: Do not convert AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) into AGREE variables—they are not in expression scope without explicit typed bindings.
Never invent or assume identifier declarations to fix unresolved references; delete the clause instead.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references require matching with clauses
AGREE annex placement for assumptions and guarantees
